perturbation.original_text: 1.9 ISA Client shall mean: (i) an entity to which Rubicon provides services pursuant to Section 3, below; and (ii) an entity that ISA has consulted with before or during the Term regarding the provision of goods or services.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.9 ISA Client shall mean: (i) an entity to which Rubicon provides services pursuant to Section 3, below; and (ii) an entity that ISA has consulted with before or during the Term regarding the provision of goods or services. For the avoidance of doubt, an ISA Client is considered a 'Customer' for the purposes of payment terms.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'For the avoidance of doubt, an ISA Client is considered a 'Customer' for the purposes of payment terms,' we are introducing potential confusion. The term 'Customer' is not defined anywhere else in the contract. This creates ambiguity, especially regarding the payment terms in Section 4, where 'ISA Client' is specifically used. This could lead to disputes about which payment terms apply to which entities, specifically since the original text refers to ISA Clients, which now may be misinterpreted to refer to Customers, due to the added perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 ISA Fees. In consideration of the services set forth in Section 2, ISA will sell or market the Rubicon Offerings at pre agreed list prices, and shall receive a commission on the gross revenues paid for the Rubicon Offerings less VAT if applicable of the Commission Rate; provided, however, that in the event that Rubicon proposes to charge at other than the Day Rate for the software development the Parties shall use all reasonable endeavours to agree an equitable adjustment to the Commission Rate.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces the term 'Customer' without defining it and equates it to 'ISA Client' for payment terms, creating ambiguity. The contradicted text (Section 4.1) refers specifically to 'ISA Fees' and commission rates for 'ISA' selling Rubicon Offerings. By stating an ISA Client is considered a 'Customer', it creates confusion as to whether Section 4.1 applies to 'Customers' as well, or if different payment terms apply, potentially contradicting the established commission structure for ISA Clients. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', since 'Customer' is not defined, and may cause conflicting obligations in terms of fees.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 ISA Fees. In consideration of the services set forth in Section 2, ISA will sell or market the Rubicon Offerings at pre agreed list prices, and shall receive a commission on the gross revenues paid for the Rubicon Offerings less VAT if applicable of the Commission Rate; provided, however, that in the event that Rubicon proposes to charge at other than the Day Rate for the software development the Parties shall use all reasonable endeavours to agree an equitable adjustment to the Commission Rate.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 ISA Fees. In consideration of the services set forth in Section 2, ISA will sell or market the Rubicon Offerings at pre agreed list prices, and shall receive a compensation on the gross revenues paid for the Rubicon Offerings less VAT if applicable of the Commission Rate; provided, however, that in the event that Rubicon proposes to charge at other than the Day Rate for the software development the Parties shall use all reasonable endeavours to agree an equitable adjustment to the Commission Rate.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes 'commission' to 'compensation'. The original contract uses 'Commission Rate' which is defined in Section 1.4. By using the term 'compensation' interchangeably with 'commission' in section 4.1, we introduce ambiguity. It's now unclear whether 'compensation' is subject to the same rate defined for 'Commission Rate', potentially leading to disputes over payment calculations. This change introduces uncertainty since the defined term 'Commission Rate' is not explicitly replaced, but rather a synonym is used in its place, creating a contradiction of which term will apply, and if the term will apply with its defined values.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.4 Commission Rate shall mean the commission rate which will be used to calculate any amount which Rubicon will pay to ISA after the provision of Rubicon Offerings to ISA Clients as set forth in Exhibit D, such rates to be subject to annual review by the Parties on the anniversary of this Agreement provided that such rates may be reviewed on an ad hoc basis at any time should the Parties become aware of specific information which impacts on the validity or feasibility of the Commission Rate.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'commission' to 'compensation' introduces ambiguity regarding whether 'compensation' is subject to the defined 'Commission Rate' in Section 1.4. The original text relies on the defined term 'Commission Rate'. By using 'compensation' interchangeably without a clear definition or replacement of 'Commission Rate', a contradiction arises, creating uncertainty and potential disputes over the applicable rate. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, causing an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 Confidentiality Obligations. Except as permitted elsewhere under this Agreement, each Party agrees (a) to take Reasonable Steps (as defined below) to receive and maintain the Confidential Information of the other Party in confidence, (b) to take Reasonable Steps (as defined below) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any third parties other than with the written consent of the disclosing Party and (c) to promptly notify the other Party upon learning of any law, rule, regulation or court order that purports to compel disclosure of any Confidential Information of the other Party and to reasonably cooperate with the other Party in the exercise of the other Party's right to protect the confidentiality of such Confidential Information, including, but not limited to, seeking to dispute the cause of such disclosure and/or to receive confidential treatment for the Confidential Information disclosed as a result of such cause. Neither Party shall use all or any part of the Confidential Information of the other Party for any purpose other than to perform its obligations, or the exercise of such Party's rights and licenses, under this Agreement. Each Party shall (i) limit access to any Confidential Information of the other Party received by it to its employees, contractors, consultants and agents who have a need-to-know in connection with the performance of such Party's obligations, or the exercise of such Party's rights, under this Agreement; and (ii) advise such employees, contractors, consultants and agents of the confidential nature thereof and of the obligations set forth in this Agreement and similarly bind them in writing. Each Party shall be responsible for any breaches of the obligations of confidentiality and restricted use set forth herein by any employee, contractor, consultant or agent to whom such Party disclosed any Confidential Information of the other Party. As used herein, "Reasonable Steps" means using at least the same degree of care that the receiving Party uses to protect its own Confidential Information, and, in any event, no less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 Confidentiality Obligations. Except as permitted elsewhere under this Agreement, each Party agrees (a) to take Reasonable Actions (as defined below) to receive and maintain the Confidential Information of the other Party in confidence, (b) to take Reasonable Actions (as defined below) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any third parties other than with the written consent of the disclosing Party and (c) to promptly notify the other Party upon learning of any law, rule, regulation or court order that purports to compel disclosure of any Confidential Information of the other Party and to reasonably cooperate with the other Party in the exercise of the other Party's right to protect the confidentiality of such Confidential Information, including, but not limited to, seeking to dispute the cause of such disclosure and/or to receive confidential treatment for the Confidential Information disclosed as a result of such cause. Neither Party shall use all or any part of the Confidential Information of the other Party for any purpose other than to perform its obligations, or the exercise of such Party's rights and licenses, under this Agreement. Each Party shall (i) limit access to any Confidential Information of the other Party received by it to its employees, contractors, consultants and agents who have a need-to-know in connection with the performance of such Party's obligations, or the exercise of such Party's rights, under this Agreement; and (ii) advise such employees, contractors, consultants and agents of the confidential nature thereof and of the obligations set forth in this Agreement and similarly bind them in writing. Each Party shall be responsible for any breaches of the obligations of confidentiality and restricted use set forth herein by any employee, contractor, consultant or agent to whom such Party disclosed any Confidential Information of the other Party. As used herein, "Reasonable Steps" means using at least the same degree of care that the receiving Party uses to protect its own Confidential Information, and, in any event, no less than reasonable care.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Reasonable Steps' to 'Reasonable Actions' but keeping the definition of 'Reasonable Steps' unchanged, we create a direct contradiction. The agreement states parties must take 'Reasonable Actions', but defines what 'Reasonable Steps' means. This contradiction introduces confusion on what standard the parties must follow regarding the confidentiality obligation. It introduces uncertainty in the enforcement of this clause since one may understand by reading, that Actions are required, but the degree of care is being defined in terms of Steps, confusing the reader and opening a door to a litigation process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 Confidentiality Obligations. Except as permitted elsewhere under this Agreement, each Party agrees (a) to take Reasonable Actions (as defined below) to receive and maintain the Confidential Information of the other Party in confidence, (b) to take Reasonable Actions (as defined below) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any third parties other than with the written consent of the disclosing Party and (c) to promptly notify the other Party upon learning of any law, rule, regulation or court order that purports to compel disclosure of any Confidential Information of the other Party and to reasonably cooperate with the other Party in the exercise of the other Party's right to protect the confidentiality of such Confidential Information, including, but not limited to, seeking to dispute the cause of such disclosure and/or to receive confidential treatment for the Confidential Information disclosed as a result of such cause. Neither Party shall use all or any part of the Confidential Information of the other Party for any purpose other than to perform its obligations, or the exercise of such Party's rights and licenses, under this Agreement. Each Party shall (i) limit access to any Confidential Information of the other Party received by it to its employees, contractors, consultants and agents who have a need-to-know in connection with the performance of such Party's obligations, or the exercise of such Party's rights, under this Agreement; and (ii) advise such employees, contractors, consultants and agents of the confidential nature thereof and of the obligations set forth in this Agreement and similarly bind them in writing. Each Party shall be responsible for any breaches of the obligations of confidentiality and restricted use set forth herein by any employee, contractor, consultant or agent to whom such Party disclosed any Confidential Information of the other Party. As used herein, "Reasonable Steps" means using at least the same degree of care that the receiving Party uses to protect its own Confidential Information, and, in any event, no less than reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Reasonable Steps' with 'Reasonable Actions,' while the definition remains for 'Reasonable Steps.' This creates a direct in-text contradiction because the agreement requires parties to take 'Reasonable Actions' but defines the standard of care as 'Reasonable Steps'. This misalignment of terminology, as defined in the Perturbation Definitions, introduces ambiguity and potential unenforceability, causing a strong contradiction within the document regarding the confidentiality obligations.
